title: Can not detect schema2-only registries, and can not convert from schema1 to schema2
body:
Pushing *some* images to a local registry fails:
```
# podman run -d -p 5000:5000 --name reg registry:2
80cd67110851f7a1691feb8f20434ccd8d87768d3fdd0d97926104597c08d7e8

# skopeo copy docker://quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest dir:/tmp/foo
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4fe2ade4980c done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

# skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false dir:/tmp/foo docker://localhost:5000/foo
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4fe2ade4980c done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Writing manifest to image destination
FATA[0000] Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to localhost:5000/foo: received unexpected HTTP status: 500 Internal Server Error 
```
skopeo-0.1.36-6.dev.gitd93a581.fc30.x86_64

--debug isn't terribly helpful, but here it is just in case:
```
...
Writing manifest to image destination
DEBU[0000] PUT http://localhost:5000/v2/foo/manifests/latest 
DEBU[0000] Writing manifest using preferred type application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws failed: Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to localhost:5000/foo: received unexpected HTTP status: 500 Internal Server Error 
FATA[0000] Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to localhost:5000/foo: received unexpected HTTP status: 500 Internal Server Error 
```

comments.body:
Simpler reproducer, without need for intermediate repository (still needs registry container running):
```
# skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false docker://quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels docker://localhost:5000/foo
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob 4fe2ade4980c done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists
Writing manifest to image destination
FATA[0001] Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to localhost:5000/foo: received unexpected HTTP status: 500 Internal Server Error 
```
Works fine with `docker://alpine` or `docker://busybox` instead of the quay.io alpine_labels image.

Thanks for your report.

This seems to work for me (when running the `registry:latest` server in Docker).

Is there anything interesting in the registry logs?

Fascinating. It doesn't work for me with `:latest` either, same symptom. Tail end of logs show:
```
10.88.0.1 - - [15/May/2019:21:08:40 +0000] "HEAD /v2/foo/blobs/sha256:a3ed95caeb02ffe68cdd9fd84406680ae93d633cb16422d00e8a7c22955b46d4 HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
time="2019-05-15T21:08:40.420395542Z" level=error msg="response completed with error" err.code=unknown err.detail="manifest schema v1 unsupported" err.message="unknown error" go.version=go1.11.2 http.request.contenttype="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws" http.request.host="localhost:5000" http.request.id=62b2d3e7-cf95-4c22-ac62-ffb0b2dbe35c http.request.method=PUT http.request.remoteaddr="10.88.0.1:45588" http.request.uri="/v2/foo/manifests/latest" http.request.useragent="Go-http-client/1.1" http.response.contenttype="application/json; charset=utf-8" http.response.duration=2.382033ms http.response.status=500 http.response.written=70 vars.name=foo vars.reference=latest
10.88.0.1 - - [15/May/2019:21:08:40 +0000] "PUT /v2/foo/manifests/latest HTTP/1.1" 500 70 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"                  
```
Ah, thanks. It turns out my `:latest` is very old, this is very likely https://github.com/docker/distribution/pull/2474 .

Unfortunately, https://github.com/docker/distribution/issues/2925 .

We _really_ should be able to autodetect this and handle it automatically. OTOH, while doing that on all of 500/“unknown error” would be possible (
```go
               if _, ok := err.(*client.UnexpectedHTTPStatusError); ok {
                       return true
               }
```
in `isManifestInvalidError`), it would also be way too loose. I’m hoping that docker/distribution defines a more specific error that we can detect in the future… though we would still need to deal with the current releases, I suppose?

---

Worse, even after doing that, copies fail with
```
Upload of manifest type application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json failed: Can not convert image to application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json, preparing DiffIDs for this case is not supported 
```
This is a c/image matter; converting from s1 to s2 requires expensive decompression of the input, so we don’t do that by default, and the current code is not ready to try s1→s1 and fall back to s1→s2.  We can easily enough do this by always decompressing blobs on copies of s1 images, but that would be very expensive if not necessary; decompression only on the s1→s2 fallback would be a much more invasive code change.

---

Right now, `skopeo copy --format=v2s2` will work around both issues, but that’s not at all a satisfactory resolution.
I think your pv registry is full of space :)
